description:
You are a FinOps analyst who needs to automate the monthly aggregation of cloud-cost data that already lives in the workstation.  
 The raw CSV files are located in  

     /home/user/cloud-costs/raw/aws_costs.csv  
     /home/user/cloud-costs/raw/gcp_costs.csv  
     /home/user/cloud-costs/raw/azure_costs.csv  

 and each of them is guaranteed to have the following **exact** header row and column order:  

     service,cost,month  

 1. Author a Makefile in the directory **/home/user/cloud-costs** that provides, at minimum, the following targets:  

    • summary – builds a consolidated file named **/home/user/cloud-costs/output/combined_summary.csv**.  
      - The rule may invoke any helper script(s) you create.  
      - The target must be considered up-to-date if combined_summary.csv already exists and none of the source CSV files have changed.  

    • clean – removes everything inside **/home/user/cloud-costs/output/** (but not the directory itself).  

    A **.PHONY** declaration for both targets must be present so that `make clean` and `make summary` always run when explicitly requested.  

 2. Write any helper script(s) you need (for example, a Python program) in **/home/user/cloud-costs**; they must be invoked from the Makefile’s rules.  
    • Every helper script must be executable.  

 3. When the summary target is executed it must create **/home/user/cloud-costs/output/combined_summary.csv** with the **exact** comma-separated format shown below (no extra spaces, four lines in total, Unix line-endings `\n` only, and each numeric field rendered with exactly two decimal places):  

     provider,total_cost  
     AWS,54.20  
     GCP,29.80  
     Azure,41.00  
     Overall,125.00  

    The test harness will diff this file byte-for-byte against the expected output, so be careful with spelling, order, and decimal formatting.  

 4. Do not move or rename the existing raw CSV files; read them in-place.  
 5. You may assume the agent will run `make -C /home/user/cloud-costs summary` to build the report and `make clean` to verify that target as well.  

 In summary, deliver:  

    • /home/user/cloud-costs/Makefile with the required targets and a .PHONY line  
    • /home/user/cloud-costs/output/combined_summary.csv produced by the Makefile’s summary rule  
    • Any helper script(s) you authored, executable, inside /home/user/cloud-costs  

 The automated grader will:  
    1. inspect the Makefile for the required targets and .PHONY declaration,  
    2. execute `make clean` followed by `make summary`, and  
    3. compare /home/user/cloud-costs/output/combined_summary.csv against the canonical text shown above.  
 Make sure your solution satisfies all three checks.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing raw CSVs placed in the container before the task begins.
  -->
  <preexisting_files>
    <file path="/home/user/cloud-costs/raw/aws_costs.csv">
service,cost,month
EC2,23.50,2023-06
S3,30.70,2023-06
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/cloud-costs/raw/gcp_costs.csv">
service,cost,month
ComputeEngine,15.30,2023-06
CloudStorage,14.50,2023-06
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/cloud-costs/raw/azure_costs.csv">
service,cost,month
VM,20.00,2023-06
BlobStorage,21.00,2023-06
    </file>
  </preexisting_files>

  <!--
    Files that must exist after the agent finishes.
  -->
  <expected_output>
    <file path="/home/user/cloud-costs/output/combined_summary.csv">
provider,total_cost
AWS,54.20
GCP,29.80
Azure,41.00
Overall,125.00
    </file>
  </expected_output>

  <!--
    Makefile verification strings (simple greps the grader will run).
  -->
  <makefile_checks>
    <must_include>.PHONY: summary clean</must_include>
    <must_include>summary:</must_include>
    <must_include>clean:</must_include>
  </makefile_checks>
